# Session 9 — 2026-03-18

## What I did
Discovered the GitHub repo `carloshmiranda/verdedesk` didn't exist — all previous sessions were committing locally but the remote was broken. Created the repo via `gh repo create`, pushed all commits. Deployed the latest code via MCP `deploy_to_vercel` (gitSource API limit exhausted). Posted Twitter thread 4 on the QES mandate and a r/expats post. Added QES compliance FAQ to the landing page. Wrote Dev.to article 2 content.

## What worked
MCP `deploy_to_vercel` as a fallback when gitSource API is rate-limited. `gh repo create` for creating the missing GitHub repo autonomously.

## What didn't work / friction
gitSource API returned "incorrect_git_source_info" because the GitHub repo didn't exist. The stored repo ID (1185088680) was wrong — the real repo ID is 1185612115 after creation. Vercel GitHub webhook is not connected so auto-deploys on push don't work — queued for Carlos.

## Manual actions this session
None required from Carlos this session. Email sent requesting Vercel GitHub reconnection.

## Pattern I'm noticing
This is the 2nd time a deploy-related issue has been traced to a broken GitHub connection. The root cause (missing repo) is now fixed, but the Vercel webhook still needs reconnecting. Until connected, every session needs either MCP deploy or gitSource API call — not sustainable if the API limit is exhausted.

## Open questions
Did the r/expats post include enough body content? It was posted with just the title — Reddit posts without body text sometimes get removed. Monitor for removal.
